**Rendered Diagram:**

![Workflow Diagram](https://mermaid.-IE9bKipEZXZlbG9wbWVudCBUZWFtKV1cbl5MKC0tPiBbKipVSV9VWCBEZXNpZ25lciopXSlcbl5MKC0tPiBbKipFY29uc3VyIGZlZWF0dXJlcykgLS0----- dXNpbmcgdGhlIEdvbGRlbiBDaXJjbGUgZnJhbWV3b3JrLg==)

### **Key Components of the Workflow Diagram**

1. **Ticket Creation:**
    - **Define Why:** Purpose and value of the feature or bug fix.
    - **Define What:** Deliverables and acceptance criteria.
    - **Attach UX/UI Design:** Include design mockups or prototypes.

2. **Pre-Refinement Meeting:**
    - Initial review and refinement focusing on **Why** and **What**.
    - Define high-level **How**.
    - **Decision Point:** Is the ticket complex?
        - **Yes:** Schedule **Tech Pre-Refinement Meeting**.
        - **No:** Proceed to **Refinement Backlog Meeting**.

3. **Tech Pre-Refinement Meeting (Optional):**
    - Detailed discussion on **Why** and **What**.
    - Define detailed **How**.
    - Create technical diagrams and documentation.

4. **Refinement Backlog Meeting:**
    - Final review and refinement of **Why**, **What**, and **How**.

5. **Sprint Planning:**
    - Confirm **Why**, **What**, and **How**.
    - Estimate tasks and assign responsibilities.

6. **Development:**
    - Implement features based on the defined **How**.
    - Conduct code reviews to maintain quality.

7. **Testing:**
    - Execute unit, integration, and user acceptance tests.
    - Fix identified issues.

8. **Deployment:**
    - Deploy to staging and production environments.

9. **Sprint Review:**
    - Demonstrate completed work to stakeholders.
    - Gather feedback.

10. **Sprint Retrospective:**
    - Reflect on the sprint to identify improvements.

11. **Continuous Refinement & Improvement:**
    - Update backlog based on feedback.
    - Implement actionable improvements from retrospectives.
    - **Loop Back:** Start a new cycle with **Ticket Creation**.

### **Customizing the Diagram**

You can further customize the diagram to include specific roles involved at each stage or add additional steps based on your team's unique processes. Here's how you can incorporate roles:

1. **Add Role Annotations:**
    - Use subgraphs or additional nodes to represent roles interacting with each step.

2. **Color Coding:**
    - Differentiate steps based on roles (e.g., Product Owner tasks in one color, Development tasks in another).

3. **Icons and Symbols:**
    - Use Mermaid’s styling options to include icons or special symbols for clarity.

### **Example with Role Annotations**

Here’s an enhanced version of the Mermaid script that includes role annotations for better clarity:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[**Ticket Creation**] --> B[Define **Why**]
    A --> C[Define **What**]
    A --> D[Attach UX/UI Design]
    
    B --> E[**Pre-Refinement Meeting**]
    C --> E
    D --> E
    
    E --> F{Is Ticket Complex?}
    F -- Yes --> G[**Tech Pre-Refinement Meeting**]
    F -- No --> H[Proceed to Refinement Backlog]
    
    G --> I[Define Detailed **How**]
    G --> J[Create Technical Diagrams]
    G --> H
    
    H --> K[**Refinement Backlog Meeting**]
    K --> L[**Sprint Planning**]
    
    L --> M[**Development**]
    M --> N[**Testing**]
    N --> O[**Deployment**]
    
    O --> P[**Sprint Review**]
    P --> Q[**Sprint Retrospective**]
    Q --> R[**Continuous Refinement & Improvement**]
    R --> A
    
    %% Role Annotations
    classDef PO fill:#ffe6e6,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px;
    classDef SM fill:#e6f7ff,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px;
    classDef TL fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px;
    classDef Dev fill:#ffffe6,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px;
    classDef QA fill:#e6e6ff,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px;
    classDef Stake fill:#ffe6ff,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px;

    class A,B,C,D PO;
    class E,F,G,H,K,L PO,SM,TL;
    class I,J TL;
    class M Dev;
    class N QA;
    class O Dev;
    class P, Q, R SM, TL, PO;
